title: Hierarchical: Meta-Level Gates Base-Level Preferences

runtime.expected_outcome: AgentPlane.Hierarchical switches the base agent's C mid-trajectory; the base agent changes direction accordingly.

symbols.C_base: base-level preference vector under current context

audiences.kid: A leader tells the worker which snack is best; when the leader changes their mind, the worker chases a different snack.
audiences.real: Hierarchical composition: the meta-agent picks a context; the base agent runs under that context's C vector. Switching context mid-episode reshapes behaviour.
audiences.equation: Base bundle.c = softmax(contexts[current_context]); switch_context mutates it.
audiences.derivation: Two-level factorisation of the generative model: p(o, s_base, s_meta) = p(o|s_base) p(s_base|s_meta) p(s_meta). Meta state acts as a prior on base-level parameters (Friston et al. 2022).

orchestrate.objective: Demonstrate AgentPlane.Hierarchical switching the base agent's C and altering behaviour.
orchestrate.context: hierarchical_maze 11x11; two contexts with different C vectors; switch every N steps.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 7.
Friston, Pezzulo, Kilner & Penny (2022), Hierarchical active inference.
